briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their purported AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse, raising urgent questions about consumer protections in embedded finance.

- **[HMRC to use AI from British tech firm to spot fraud and tax return errors](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c7v9ld262n4o?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — UK tax authority HMRC has awarded a £175M contract to financial data platform Quantexa to deploy AI in detecting fraud and errors in tax returns.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A detailed analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of UK open banking startup Credit Kudos signals growing big-tech interest in leveraging financial data for consumer credit and payments services.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Are Canadians reaching their 'breaking point'? New data shows more people filing for insolvency](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/insolvency-filings-increasing-canada-9.7197230?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — New data reveals a rising tide of insolvency filings across Canada, pointing to growing financial stress among households and raising financial inclusion concerns.

- **[Canadian VC sees lowest quarterly deal count in nearly a decade](https://betakit.com/canadian-vc-sees-lowest-quarterly-deal-count-in-nearly-a-decade/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's venture capital market recorded its lowest quarterly deal count in nearly ten years, signalling a challenging funding environment for startups including fintechs.

- **[Top Down closes VC fund aimed at "invisible infrastructure" of the global economy](https://betakit.com/top-down-closes-vc-fund-aimed-at-invisible-infrastructure-of-the-global-economy/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian VC firm Top Down has closed a new fund targeting foundational "invisible infrastructure" companies, a category that includes fintech and payments rails.

- **[Ian Crosby is back with a new accounting startup for the agentic AI era](https://betakit.com/ian-crosby-is-back-with-a-new-accounting-startup-for-the-agentic-ai-era/)** — *BetaKit* — The co-founder of Bench Accounting has launched a new AI-native accounting startup, betting that agentic AI will transform financial back-office operations for businesses.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada flagged security risks in screen scraping and committed to an open banking framework, a foundational milestone relevant to tracking the country's ongoing consumer-directed finance progress.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canada wants sovereign AI data centres. What does that actually mean?](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bakx-canada-sovereign-ai-9.7198649?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's push for sovereign AI data infrastructure raises important questions about data residency and security that directly affect fintech platforms handling sensitive financial data, with potential implications for Atlantic Canadian tech hubs.

- **[Feds announce $66 million for 44 businesses through AI Compute Access Fund](https://betakit.com/feds-announce-66-million-for-44-businesses-through-ai-compute-access-fund/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government's $66M AI Compute Access Fund represents a potential funding pathway for Atlantic Canadian fintech and tech startups looking to integrate AI into financial products and services.
